questions.question: Evaluate the expression: $(-7) + (-9)$
questions.explanation: Since both numbers have the same sign (negative), we add their absolute values ($7 + 9 = 16$) and keep the negative sign, yielding $-16$.

questions.question: Solve the subtraction problem: $4 - (-5)$
questions.explanation: Subtracting a negative number is equivalent to adding its positive opposite: $4 - (-5) = 4 + 5 = 9$.

questions.question: What is the product of $(-8)$ and $(-4)$?
questions.explanation: Multiplying two negative numbers (same signs) always results in a positive product: $(-8) \times (-4) = 32$.

questions.question: Evaluate the quotient: $(-42) \div (+6)$
questions.explanation: Dividing two numbers with different signs results in a negative quotient: $42 \div 6 = 7$, so the answer is $-7$.
